name: 크롬과 엣지 브라우저 접근 권한 차이

chat_messages.text:
https://www.genspark.ai/api/files/s/LqStnA2D
주소의 내용이 크롬에서는 활성화되는데
엣지에서는 아래와 같은 에러 발생, 원인확인 및 대처방안 제안 부탁

```
{"detail":"Access denied","reason":"Backend denied access"}
```

chat_messages.text:

CSP, 이 문제의 원인과 대처방안을 정리해드리겠습니다.

해당 URL(`genspark.ai/api/files/s/...`)은 Genspark의 파일 공유 API 엔드포인트인데, 제가 직접 접근했을 때도 403 에러가 발생했습니다. 즉 이 URL은 인증된 세션이 있어야만 콘텐츠를 내려주는 구조입니다.

**크롬에서는 되고 엣지에서는 안 되는 핵심 원인은 "인증 세션(쿠키) 차이"입니다.**

Genspark은 로그인 후 세션 쿠키를 기반으로 API 접근 권한을 부여합니다. 크롬에서는 Genspark에 로그인된 상태의 쿠키가 살아 있어서 `/api/files/` 엔드포인트에 접근할 때 해당 쿠키가 함께 전송되고, 서버가 이를 인증된 요청으로 인식합니다. 반면 엣지에서는 Genspark에 로그인하지 않았거나, 로그인했더라도 쿠키가 제대로 유지되지 않아서 서버가 `"Backend denied access"`를 반환하는 것입니다.

특히 2026년 현재 브라우저들의 서드파티 쿠키 정책이 강화되면서, Genspark 같은 플랫폼의 크로스 도메인 인증 플로우가 브라우저별로 다르게 동작하는 경우가 빈번합니다.

**구체적으로 의심되는 원인들은 다음과 같습니다.**

첫째, 엣지에서 Genspark에 로그인되어 있지 않거나, 로그인 세션이 만료된 상태입니다. 이 API 엔드포인트는 공개 URL처럼 보이지만 실제로는 인증이 필요한 보호된 리소스입니다.

둘째, 엣지의 프라이버시 설정이 크롬보다 더 엄격하게 서드파티 쿠키를 차단하고 있을 수 있습니다. 특히 "추적 방지" 설정이 "엄격"으로 되어 있으면 인증에 필요한 쿠키가 차단됩니다.

셋째, 광고 차단기나 프라이버시 확장 프로그램이 엣지에 설치되어 있다면, 이들이 필수 스크립트나 쿠키를 차단하여 접근이 거부될 수 있습니다.

**대처 방안은 이렇습니다.**

**1단계 — 엣지에서 Genspark 로그인 확인**: `genspark.ai`에 접속해서 로그인 상태인지 확인하고, 로그인 후 해당 URL을 다시 시도합니다. 이것만으로 해결될 가능성이 가장 높습니다.

**2단계 — 추적 방지 수준 조정**: 엣지 `설정 > 개인 정보, 검색 및 서비스 > 추적 방지`에서 "기본" 또는 "균형 잡힌"으로 설정합니다. "엄격"이면 Genspark의 인증 쿠키가 차단될 수 있습니다.

**3단계 — Genspark 도메인 쿠키 예외 추가**: 엣지 `설정 > 쿠키 및 사이트 권한`에서 `genspark.ai`를 허용 목록에 추가합니다.

**4단계 — 엣지 캐시/쿠키 초기화 후 재로그인**: 엣지의 캐시와 쿠키를 삭제하면 손상된 데이터가 제거되어 접근 거부 에러가 해결될 수 있습니다. 초기화 후 Genspark에 다시 로그인하면 됩니다.

**5단계 — InPrivate 모드 테스트**: 엣지에서 `Ctrl+Shift+N`으로 InPrivate 창을 열고, Genspark에 로그인한 뒤 해당 URL을 시도합니다. 이렇게 하면 확장 프로그램이나 기존 쿠키 충돌 없이 테스트할 수 있습니다.

결론적으로, 이 에러는 Genspark 서버가 "너 누구야? 인증 안 됐어"라고 말하는 것이지, 엣지 자체의 결함이 아닙니다. 크롬에서는 이미 로그인된 세션 쿠키가 동행하고 있을 뿐입니다. 가장 빠른 해결은 엣지에서 Genspark에 로그인한 상태로 해당 URL에 접근하는 것입니다.

content.title: Edgesuite.net Access Denied Error When Visiting Web sites – Adam Leinss' Tech Tips
content.text:
One of these request headers is the “User-Agent” header which tells the remote server about the browser software and its version installed on the client. It looked like edgesuite.net denies access from my very outdated Android Firefox version. I could have updated the Firefox app, instead I didn’t do that but rather entered “about:config” in the URL bar, added a new string type configuration variable called “general.useragent.override” and added the value of the most current Linux Firefox useragent string to it, e.g. “Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux amd64; rv:137.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/138.0”, and suddenly the edgesuite.net access denied errors were gone! Note: The general.useragent.override setting is a Firefox specific configuration option to override the default User-Agent string sent by the browser which reflects the real version of the browser.

I think edgesuite.net bans older browsers for security reasons, the exact algorithm they use for banning clients is of course not known to the public. And for sure, checking the User-Agent string is just one of many security measures applied by edgesuite.net, but technically they can’t do much more than blacklist IP addresses and evaluate client request HTTP header fields. Please also note that in my example with an android device I had maybe better taken another UserAgent string from an Android or other mobile device, because changing the User-Agent string might also lead to a diffrent rendering of websites, which might not be in the user’s interest when working with small displays like on a smartphone. ... The last two extensions I tried was the same guy who hung up on me . ... ugh, i’ve seen that access denied error before, super annoying.

Edgesuite appears to be a company determined to prevent shoppers from accessing websites. I have tried three browsers and still cannot access an art supply website. I wonder how much money Michaels.com loses annually because shoppers are denied access and go elsewhere? ... Thanks for your thorough post. Yesterday I posted a one-star review of our new Samsung TV. Today, clicking a Samsung broadcast-email link, with my S24, I get: — Access Denied You don’t have permission to access “http://www.samsung.com/us/tvs/qled-tv/highlights/?” on this server. Reference #18.8….. 248 https://errors.edgesuite.net/18.8… 248 — I checked McDonald’s and Costco as well as other sites with no problems. ... Did not intend to publish my full name on the comment. Please do not use it. ... Removed last name. ... It looks like edgesuite.net bans clients by various information submitted in the HTTP GET request header, submitted on each page access, besides other things like the IP address of the client.

One of these request headers is the “User-Agent” header which tells the remote server about the browser software and its version installed on the client. It looked like edgesuite.net denies access from my very outdated Android Firefox version. I could have updated the Firefox app, instead I didn’t do that but rather entered “about:config” in the URL bar, added a new string type configuration variable called “general.useragent.override” and added the value of the most current Linux Firefox useragent string to it, e.g. “Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux amd64; rv:137.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/138.0”, and suddenly the edgesuite.net access denied errors were gone! Note: The general.useragent.override setting is a Firefox specific configuration option to override the default User-Agent string sent by the browser which reflects the real version of the browser.

It appears after 4 days of removing several Chrome extensions I wasn’t actively using, it’s finally resolved. I suspect it may have to due to 2 Chrome extensions I had loaded in the past: one called “DownloadThemAll!” and another called “Video Download Helper”. I had used DownloadThemAll! to download some audio files from a website (freely available on said website) several months ago. I didn’t want to click 60+ individual links to download them, I wanted to click once and do it batch mode style. My theory is one of the websites I visit was using Akamai’s CDN service saw these extensions loaded in my browser and just assumed I was a bad actor, even though I wasn’t actively using the tools on their website and added me to a naughty list, which was then shared to the rest of Akamai’s customers and thus I was blocked from many websites.

Edgesuite.net Access Denied Error When Visiting Web sites · Buckle up, this is going to be an interesting one. A few weeks ago, I started getting errors like this visiting web sites like McDonalds, Costco, Meijer, Pick ‘n Save: Access Denied You don’t have permission to access “http://www.costco.com/?” on this server. ... Odd. I started looking around on the Internet which lead me to this thread on the Verizon FIOS site: https://community.verizon.com/t5/Fios-Internet-and-High-Speed/multiple-websites-quot-access-denied-quot-over-verizon-Fios/td-p/1746618 · Reading through the thread, I found a post by smith6612 pointing to this link: https://www.akamai.com/us/en/clientrep-lookup/. I went and checked my IP address: it was listed as a web scrapper. These companies use Akamai as a CDN (Content Distribution Network) and according to the store IT support, my IP address was listed as a 10/10 or a very bad IP address in Akamai.

After contacting Spectrum technical support, they suggested a get a new cable modem which should give me a new IP address. I did so and….same IP address. ARGH! Anyways, I was already prepared with a plan B: it appears that if you change the MAC access of your router slightly, even by 1 character, Spectrum will see this as a new router and will give you a different IP address. I changed the MAC address on the router and restarted the router and the cable modem: I was granted a new IP address! This new IP address was not blacklisted by Akamai. The option to change a MAC address will vary by router model, but the TP-LINK AX5400 lets you change the MAC address in the router’s web GUI. The other option suggested by Spectrum was to leave the cable modem turned off for 24 hours (for the DHCP lease to expire) and then plug it back in, but that option may not be viable for many people unless you have some type of backup Internet option like a hotspot.

content.title: Fix STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED Microsoft Edge error
content.text:
So you need to clear the Edge cache. If you cannot access Edge settings, then use a tool like CCleaner to delete the browser cache. To clear cookies and cache in Microsoft Edge, use the steps below- While on Edge, go to the top-right corner ...

Some Edge users have reported that they get the STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED error when they try to access any web address and are even unable to open Edge settings. This is annoying because it blocks you from visiting any website, and you cannot even tweak the settings. In this post, we will look at ways to fix the STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED Microsoft Edge error. Edge is one of the most popular browsers liked by many users. So when an issue or bug affects it, it affects a large number of people. The STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED error doesn’t indicate that your PC has issues; it is a problem to do with Microsoft Edge. Luckily we have the best solutions that will fix the browser error and resume your browsing. If the access is denied in Microsoft Edge, it means that you can use the browser to access any URL. The causes for this error may range from issues with Edge access and permissions privileges or corrupted Edge files, incorrect system settings, or compatibility issues with third-party applications like 

If the cookies and cache in Microsoft Edge are not working properly, probably they are corrupted and will definitely trigger the STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED error. So you need to clear the Edge cache. If you cannot access Edge settings, then use a tool like CCleaner to delete the browser cache. To clear cookies and cache in Microsoft Edge, use the steps below- While on Edge, go to the top-right corner of the browser and select the three dots. You will see several options; locate Settings and click on it. You will see more options on the left side of the settings window. Go to Privacy, search, and services. On the left side, go to the Clear browsing data option and select Choose what to clear which is next to the Clear browsing data now option. Select All time at the top drop-down menu and check all boxes next to all options in the new window. Choose Clear now to start the process of clearing cache and cookies.

Once the process is over, relaunch your browser and see if you are still getting the STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED error. Related: STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION error in Chrome or Edge · Reset Microsoft Edge and see if that helps. Remember to back up your browser Favorites, etc. before you do so. If you cannot access Edge settings, then use the Repair option in Windows Settings. Deleting Edge-related files in Local disk (C:), ensure that you completely remove all Edge data, and when you reinstall it, there will be no conflicts. To delete Edge-related files and reinstall it, follow the steps below; Open the Run dialog box by pressing the Windows button + R, they type or copy and paste the following file path and hit Enter on your keyboard: ... The command will open the Microsoft folder that hosts app folders like Edge, Teams, Word, etc. Locate the Edge folder and right-click on it.

Next is to reinstall Edge. You can use another browser to access the official download page for Edge or you can use Microsoft Store to search and install Edge. You can also get MicrosoftEdgeSetup.exe installation file from another computer using a portable disk. We hope one of the solutions helps you fix the STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED Microsoft Edge error. Read: Cannot Download, Install or Update Edge browser · To clear your Microsoft Edge settings, open Edge settings and go to Privacy, search, and services on the left side. Locate the Clear browsing data and click on Choose what to clear. Select the time range and tick the box next to what you want to delete and then select Clear now. If you only want to clear data from your current device, log out first from Edge before you click Clear now, other you will clear data from all synced devices. Fix: Microsoft Edge Installation & Update Error Codes ·

content.title: Access Denied on This Server: Causes and Step-by-Step Fixes - UptimeRobot Knowledge Hub
content.text:
Firefox: Navigate to Settings > Privacy & Security > Clear Cookies and Site Data. Edge: Access Settings > Privacy, Search, and Services > Clear browsing data. Some websites restrict access to traffic coming from VPNs or proxies.

Sometimes, your browser stores outdated or corrupted cookies and cache, which can prevent access to certain websites. Clearing this data can resolve the issue quickly. Google Chrome: Go to Settings > Privacy and Security > Clear browsing data. Firefox: Navigate to Settings > Privacy & Security > Clear Cookies and Site Data. Edge: Access Settings > Privacy, Search, and Services > Clear browsing data. Some websites restrict access to traffic coming from VPNs or proxies. If you’re using one, disabling it may solve the problem. Windows: Go to Settings > Network & Internet > Proxy and turn off automatic proxy setup. macOS: Open System Preferences > Network > Advanced > Proxies and disable proxy settings. Mobile Devices: Turn off VPN via the device’s settings menu. Certain websites enforce geo-restrictions, meaning access is blocked based on your location.

Encountering an “Access Denied on This Server” error can be frustrating, especially when you need to access important information. This error occurs when a server blocks your request, preventing you from viewing a website. Common causes include IP blocking, browser issues, security software settings, and VPN or proxy interference. In this guide, we’ll break down the reasons behind this error and provide clear, actionable steps to resolve it. Bookmark this page for future reference in case you run into this problem again. 💡TIP: Catch website or server errors before they turn into bigger incidents. Set up free uptime monitoring in just a few clicks. Downtime happens. Get notified! Join the world's leading uptime monitoring service with 3.2M+ happy users. ... In simple terms, the “Access Denied on This Server” error means the server hosting the website has refused your request to view the page.

Browser extensions, such as ad blockers or privacy tools, can sometimes interfere with website permissions. For example: Ad Blockers: Some websites detect ad blockers and deny access until they’re disabled. Privacy Extensions: Tools like Privacy Badger or uBlock Origin might block essential scripts, causing the site to malfunction. 📍Real-World Example: A user with an ad blocker enabled tries to access a news website but is met with an “Access Denied” message. Disabling the ad blocker allows them to view the content. VPNs and proxies are popular tools for enhancing privacy and bypassing restrictions, but they can also trigger access denials. Here’s why: Many websites block traffic from known VPN servers to prevent users from circumventing geo-restrictions or hiding their identities. For example: Streaming Platforms: Services like Netflix actively block VPN traffic to enforce regional licensing agreements.

Keep your browser and operating system updated: Outdated software can introduce compatibility issues that cause access restrictions. Enable automatic updates to stay ahead of potential problems. Use a reliable VPN service: Free VPNs often use shared IPs that are frequently blacklisted by websites. A premium VPN with dedicated or rotating IPs helps reduce access issues while maintaining privacy. Monitor website availability with downtime checkers: Before troubleshooting, confirm whether the site itself is down using UptimeRobot or simple tools like Down For Everyone Or Just Me. This helps differentiate between a personal access issue and a broader server problem. Adjust firewall and security software settings proactively: Some security programs may mistakenly block safe websites. Periodically reviewing and updating security settings can prevent unnecessary restrictions. The “Access denied on this server” error can be a hassle, but it’s usually fixable with the right steps.

content.title: Fix: STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED on Microsoft Edge
content.text:
Clearing the browser cache and cookies will delete all corrupt data and files and fix the STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED error in Microsoft Edge. In addition, do not hesitate to check our guide about how to clear the computer cache in Windows 11.

The STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED error in Microsoft Edge indicates the browser can’t access a specific resource or perform some tasks. It can occur due to restricted permissions and corrupt or misconfigured browser settings. You can resolve the error by clearing Edge caches and cookies or deleting Edge-related folders and reinstalling the browser. Many users have reported an error STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED in Microsoft Edge that appears when using the browser. It occurs when a permission issue prevents the browser from performing a particular action. Hence, this article will discuss how to fix the problem. Alternatively, you can check our article about fixing Microsoft Edge not connecting to the Internet on your PC. Microsoft Edge might encounter Access Denied error if the UAC settings are high. Not having the necessary permissions, such as administrative privileges or file/folder access, could prompt the errors.

Misconfigured settings within Microsoft Edge or corrupted browser data can cause this error. Windows or Microsoft Edge updates can install new features or changes that affect access permissions. Network restrictions set by network administrators can also cause access-denied errors in Microsoft Edge. Before attempting any advanced troubleshooting steps, we recommend you go through the following preliminary checks: Run Microsoft Edge as an administrator to give the browser elevated permissions. Temporarily disable antivirus or firewall software to fix the error. Update Microsoft Edge and Windows OS to resolve this issue. Launch the Microsoft Edge app on your computer. Further, click on the three dots on the top right side and select Settings. Now, select the Privacy, search, and services option on the left side panel menu, Further, navigate to the Clear browsing data section, and select the Choose what to clear option.

Clearing the browser cache and cookies will delete all corrupt data and files and fix the STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED error in Microsoft Edge. In addition, do not hesitate to check our guide about how to clear the computer cache in Windows 11. Launch the Microsoft Edge app on your computer. Click on the three dots on the top right side and select Settings. Scroll down, click on the Reset settings option, and click Restore settings to their default values. Click on the Reset button. After the reset is complete, Microsoft Edge will open with its default settings restored. Resetting the browser will resolve issues related to misconfigured settings and other underlying problems. Hence, it fixes the STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED error in Microsoft Edge. ... Press the Windows + E key to open File Explorer. Now, navigate to the following directory: C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft\ Locate the Edge and EdgeCore folders, right-click on them, and select Delete from the drop-down menu.

Research from 2026 indicates that 23% of authentication loops result from browser third-party cookie restrictions. Modern privacy-focused browsers like Safari and Firefox block these by default, disrupting cross-domain authentication flows essential for platforms like Genspark. ... Privacy Note: Enabling third-party cookies for specific domains maintains overall browser security while allowing necessary authentication. This targeted approach, recommended by security experts, prevents blanket security reductions. ... Third-party cookie deprecation presents a fundamental shift in web authentication architecture. Platforms must transition to Federated Credential Management (FedCM) APIs and privacy-preserving alternatives like Storage Access API to maintain seamless user experiences beyond 2026. — Marcus Chen, Lead Security Architect at W3C Web Authentication Working Group, 2026 ·

Industry experts predict authentication challenges will intensify as browsers complete third-party cookie deprecation throughout 2026. Google Chrome plans to offer user-controlled cookie blocking rather than automatic deprecation, while Safari and Firefox already block by default. The European Commission’s proposed mandatory one-click reject buttons will further restrict cookie usage. However, modern platforms like Genspark are transitioning to Federated Credential Management (FedCM) APIs and privacy-preserving authentication alternatives that don’t rely on third-party cookies. Users should expect authentication methods to evolve but remain functional as platforms adapt to the cookieless future. ... Yes, VPN services contribute to 8.7% of authentication loop cases according to research data. VPNs can cause authentication failures through three mechanisms: (1) IP address changes during login confuse session validation; (2) VPN servers may block authentication API endpoints as part of th

The Genspark engineering team resolved the server-side component within 48 hours, but client-side cookie conflicts continue to affect users in 2026. 🔄 Endless redirect cycle after email submission · ⚠️ Login page reappears immediately after attempting to sign in · ❌ No error messages displayed, creating confusion about the root cause · 🌐 Issue persists across multiple browsers (Chrome, Edge, Firefox, Safari) 📱 Affects both desktop and mobile access points simultaneously · ⏰ Temporary success followed by immediate failure in subsequent login attempts · Research shows that authentication loops result from complex interactions between browser security policies, cookie management, and authentication server configurations.

By default, unauthenticated and unauthorized requests made to known API endpoints protected by cookie authentication now result in 401 and 403 responses rather than redirecting to a login or access-denied URI. Known API endpoints are identified using the new IApiEndpointMetadata interface, and metadata implementing the new interface has been added automatically to the following: ... Minimal API endpoints that read JSON request bodies or write JSON responses. Endpoints using TypedResults return types. SignalR endpoints. ... Previously, the cookie authentication handler redirected unauthenticated and unauthorized requests to a login or access-denied URI by default for all requests other than XMLHttpRequests (XHRs).

content.text:
Does a "Cookie Check Failed" error keep interrupting your browsing experience? This quick guide explains the step-by-step solutions to get your cookie authentication working again. ... Encountering a "Cookie Check Failed" error while browsing can be both surprising and frustrating. Suddenly, you can't log in, make purchases, or even access personalized content - and you're left wondering what went wrong. If you're looking for solutions, you're on the right page. In this quick guide, we'll share tips and troubleshooting steps to help you resolve this error. Read on! Cookies, or browser cookies, are small data files containing some of your information like login details and browsing preferences. They are temporarily stored on your web browser to help websites recognize you when you return. While they might seem like a minor detail, cookies play an important role in providing you with a smooth and personalized online experience.

Cookies, or browser cookies, are small data files containing some of your information like login details and browsing preferences. They are temporarily stored on your web browser to help websites recognize you when you return. While they might seem like a minor detail, cookies play an important role in providing you with a smooth and personalized online experience.

WordPress has a security feature called "nonce" that generates these authentication cookies for the REST API to protect against cross-site request forgery (CSRF) attacks. A nonce, or "number used once," is assigned to each request and expires after a set time. However, if this nonce value gets saved in the cache for too long, it could potentially be used for malicious requests. That is why website owners need to set up rules to automatically get rid of these cached nonce values after a certain amount of time. Doing so will not only reduce security risks but also prevent failed authentication when a legitimate user visits the web. A "Cookie Check Failed" error essentially means that the website is having trouble reading or accessing the cookies on your browser. This can lead to problems like failed login attempts or the inability to display personalized content based on your preferences.

In the case of WordPress, this error indicates that the platform is struggling to verify or set cookies, which are important for user authentication and session management. Without properly functioning cookies, WordPress can't reliably identify users or maintain their active sessions. ... This error often occurs when the user's browser doesn't accept or has disabled cookies. If cookies are blocked or turned off, WordPress can't operate correctly. Like any other data file, cookies can sometimes get corrupted or garbled, making them unreadable. As a result, the site can't properly authenticate you or retrieve your user data stored in those cookies. In some cases, cookies can also get deleted prematurely before reaching their expiration date. This might happen if you manually clear your browsing data or if your browser is set to automatically remove cookies periodically.

content.text:
One of these 4 ‘low privilege’ folders, used by the browser in Windows, in the course of daily operation, is Cookies, the other being Cache, History & Temp, and it is located at: ... With the Protected Mode turned On, the browser essentially runs as a low privilege process; as a result of which it can store/read/write cookies in the LOW version of the Cookies folder: C:\Users\username\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Cookies\Low · But if you have turned UAC off or Disabled the Protected Mode in Windows, they (like cache, temp & history) will primarily be stored in: C:\Users\username\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Cookies · Read: Configure how Microsoft Edge treats Cookies in Windows. This post will tell you about the types of Internet Cookies, should you be interested. Also read: Where are Temporary Internet Files stored in Windows 11? Usually, no, but if you set up the permissions correctly, you will be able to do so.

TheWindowsClub covers authentic Windows 11, Windows 10 tips, tutorials, how-to's, features, freeware. Created by Anand Khanse, MVP. ... Where are Cookies stored by Chrome, Edge, Firefox, and Opera browsers in Windows 11/10? Where is the location of the Cookies folder? In this post, we will show you the location of the Cookies folder. Starting with Windows Vista, things changed. If you typed Cookies in Start Menu, it took you to the C:\Users\<Username>\Cookies folder. When you tried to click on it, in all probability, you would be greeted with an Access Denied box. This path, however, is just a kind of a pointer. Google Chrome stores Cookies in Windows 11/10 in the following folder location: %LOCALAPPDATA%\Google\Chrome\User Data\Default\Network · Mozilla Firefox stores Cookies in a single file called cookies.sqlite. Go to the following path in Explorer: ...

Most websites and apps use cookies for authentication and to personalize your experience with the website. Without cookies, it will be difficult to authenticate your account, and you will not be able to get recommendations. Anand Khanse is the Admin of TheWindowsClub.com, a 10-year Microsoft MVP (2006-16) & a Windows Insider MVP (2016-2022).

In Windows 8 and Windows 8.1, the Cookies are stored by IE in this folder: C:\Users\username\AppData\Local\Microsoft\Windows\INetCookies · In Windows 11 and Windows 10, if open the Run box, type shell:cookies, and press the Enter key to open the Cookies folder, the following will open: C:\Users\username\AppData\Local\Microsoft\Windows\INetCookies · But this location is deprecated now. As mentioned elsewhere on this site, starting with Windows Vista, processes run with integrity levels as defined by the Mandatory Integrity Control feature. Internet Explorer in Protected Mode runs as a ‘Low Privilege’ process. This prevents Microsoft Edge from writing to areas of the File System or the Registry that require a higher privilege! Windows creates a set of folders and files for use with Protected Mode in Microsoft Edge. These folders and files share the same Low Privilege level as Microsoft Edge.

One of these 4 ‘low privilege’ folders, used by the browser in Windows, in the course of daily operation, is Cookies, the other being Cache, History & Temp, and it is located at: ... With the Protected Mode turned On, the browser essentially runs as a low privilege process; as a result of which it can store/read/write cookies in the LOW version of the Cookies folder: C:\Users\username\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Cookies\Low · But if you have turned UAC off or Disabled the Protected Mode in Windows, they (like cache, temp & history) will primarily be stored in: C:\Users\username\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Cookies · Read: Configure how Microsoft Edge treats Cookies in Windows. Some of the cookies are common on all requests. Other cookies are used for specific authentication flows or specific client-side conditions. Persistent session tokens are stored as persistent cookies on the web browser's cookie jar. Non-persistent session tokens are stored as session cookies on the web browser, and are destroyed when the browser session is closed. ... Cookies identified as client-side cookies are set locally on the client device by JavaScript, hence, will be marked with HttpOnly=false.

해당 URL(`genspark.ai/api/files/s/...`)은 Genspark의 파일 공유 API 엔드포인트인데, 제가 직접 접근했을 때도 403 에러가 발생했습니다. 즉 이 URL은 인증된 세션이 있어야만 콘텐츠를 내려주는 구조입니다.

**크롬에서는 되고 엣지에서는 안 되는 핵심 원인은 "인증 세션(쿠키) 차이"입니다.**

Genspark은 로그인 후 세션 쿠키를 기반으로 API 접근 권한을 부여합니다. 크롬에서는 Genspark에 로그인된 상태의 쿠키가 살아 있어서 `/api/files/` 엔드포인트에 접근할 때 해당 쿠키가 함께 전송되고, 서버가 이를 인증된 요청으로 인식합니다. 반면 엣지에서는 Genspark에 로그인하지 않았거나, 로그인했더라도 쿠키가 제대로 유지되지 않아서 서버가 `"Backend denied access"`를 반환하는 것입니다.

특히 2026년 현재 브라우저들의 서드파티 쿠키 정책이 강화되면서, Genspark 같은 플랫폼의 크로스 도메인 인증 플로우가 브라우저별로 다르게 동작하는 경우가 빈번합니다.

**구체적으로 의심되는 원인들은 다음과 같습니다.**

첫째, 엣지에서 Genspark에 로그인되어 있지 않거나, 로그인 세션이 만료된 상태입니다. 이 API 엔드포인트는 공개 URL처럼 보이지만 실제로는 인증이 필요한 보호된 리소스입니다.

둘째, 엣지의 프라이버시 설정이 크롬보다 더 엄격하게 서드파티 쿠키를 차단하고 있을 수 있습니다. 특히 "추적 방지" 설정이 "엄격"으로 되어 있으면 인증에 필요한 쿠키가 차단됩니다.

셋째, 광고 차단기나 프라이버시 확장 프로그램이 엣지에 설치되어 있다면, 이들이 필수 스크립트나 쿠키를 차단하여 접근이 거부될 수 있습니다.

**대처 방안은 이렇습니다.**

**1단계 — 엣지에서 Genspark 로그인 확인**: `genspark.ai`에 접속해서 로그인 상태인지 확인하고, 로그인 후 해당 URL을 다시 시도합니다. 이것만으로 해결될 가능성이 가장 높습니다.

**2단계 — 추적 방지 수준 조정**: 엣지 `설정 > 개인 정보, 검색 및 서비스 > 추적 방지`에서 "기본" 또는 "균형 잡힌"으로 설정합니다. "엄격"이면 Genspark의 인증 쿠키가 차단될 수 있습니다.

**3단계 — Genspark 도메인 쿠키 예외 추가**: 엣지 `설정 > 쿠키 및 사이트 권한`에서 `genspark.ai`를 허용 목록에 추가합니다.

**4단계 — 엣지 캐시/쿠키 초기화 후 재로그인**: 엣지의 캐시와 쿠키를 삭제하면 손상된 데이터가 제거되어 접근 거부 에러가 해결될 수 있습니다. 초기화 후 Genspark에 다시 로그인하면 됩니다.

**5단계 — InPrivate 모드 테스트**: 엣지에서 `Ctrl+Shift+N`으로 InPrivate 창을 열고, Genspark에 로그인한 뒤 해당 URL을 시도합니다. 이렇게 하면 확장 프로그램이나 기존 쿠키 충돌 없이 테스트할 수 있습니다.

결론적으로, 이 에러는 Genspark 서버가 "너 누구야? 인증 안 됐어"라고 말하는 것이지, 엣지 자체의 결함이 아닙니다. 크롬에서는 이미 로그인된 세션 쿠키가 동행하고 있을 뿐입니다. 가장 빠른 해결은 엣지에서 Genspark에 로그인한 상태로 해당 URL에 접근하는 것입니다.
젠스파크 url을 마스킹하는 방법

CSP, 질문의 맥락을 좀 더 정확히 이해하고 싶습니다. "Genspark URL을 마스킹한다"는 것이 몇 가지 다른 의미로 해석될 수 있어서요.

content.text: CSP, 질문의 맥락을 좀 더 정확히 이해하고 싶습니다. "Genspark URL을 마스킹한다"는 것이 몇 가지 다른 의미로 해석될 수 있어서요.

questions.question: 어떤 목적으로 URL을 마스킹하려는 건가요?

chat_messages.text:
Q: 어떤 목적으로 URL을 마스킹하려는 건가요?
A: 공유 시 원본 URL 숨기기 (짧은 링크/커스텀 도메인)

content.text:
Q: 어떤 목적으로 URL을 마스킹하려는 건가요?
A: 공유 시 원본 URL 숨기기 (짧은 링크/커스텀 도메인)

chat_messages.text:
Genspark 파일 URL을 공유할 때 원본 주소를 숨기는 방법, 몇 가지 접근법을 정리해드리겠습니다.

## 1. 단축 URL 서비스 활용 (가장 간편)

**Bitly, Dub.co, Short.io** 같은 서비스에서 `https://www.genspark.ai/api/files/s/LqStnA2D`를 입력하면 `https://bit.ly/abc123` 같은 형태로 변환됩니다. 다만 주의할 점이 있습니다 — 앞서 확인한 것처럼 이 URL은 **인증된 세션 쿠키가 있어야 접근 가능한 보호된 엔드포인트**입니다. 단축 URL로 감싸더라도 받는 사람이 Genspark에 로그인되어 있지 않으면 동일한 `Access denied` 에러를 만나게 됩니다. 즉, URL 마스킹은 되지만 **접근성 문제는 별도로 해결해야** 합니다.

## 2. 커스텀 도메인 리다이렉트 (자체 도메인 보유 시)

CSP가 개인 도메인을 갖고 있다면, Cloudflare Workers나 Vercel Edge Functions로 간단한 리다이렉트 프록시를 만들 수 있습니다. 예를 들어 `https://share.csp.dev/file/abc` → Genspark URL로 302 리다이렉트하는 방식입니다. 이렇게 하면 받는 사람에게는 CSP의 도메인만 노출되고 Genspark이라는 서비스명은 완전히 숨겨집니다.

## 3. 파일 자체를 중계하는 방식 (가장 확실)

인증 문제까지 동시에 해결하려면, Genspark에서 파일을 다운로드한 뒤 **별도의 공유 가능한 저장소에 업로드**하는 것이 가장 깔끔합니다. Google Drive, SharePoint, Notion 페이지 첨부, 혹은 Supabase Storage 같은 곳에 올리면 원본 출처를 완전히 숨기면서도 수신자의 인증 문제를 원천적으로 제거할 수 있습니다.

## 실용적 권장

사내 공유가 목적이라면 **3번(파일 중계)**이 가장 현실적입니다. Genspark URL 자체가 세션 의존적이라 단축 URL로 감싸도 받는 사람 입장에서는 열리지 않을 확률이 높기 때문입니다. 외부 서비스명 노출 방지가 핵심 관심사라면, 파일을 꺼내서 사내 인프라(SharePoint 등)에 재배치하는 것이 URL 마스킹과 접근성을 동시에 해결하는 유일한 방법입니다.

혹시 특정 구현 방식(예: Vercel 리다이렉트 프록시 코드, Supabase Storage 업로드 자동화 등)으로 더 들어가고 싶으시면 말씀해주세요.
